pageCaption.value: Since 2017, the Coral Gardeners collective has restored and monitored more than 30,000 corals. Founder Titouan Bernicot has no formal training in marine biology but the reef is his life. The organization has recruited experts and enthusiastic people willing to help. As a result, the team refines its methods and plants more corals every year. All the while, it is telling the “story of the reef” and has already reached more than 200 million people worldwide.

metadescription.value: With its dynamic team of scientists, creators and advocates, Coral Gardeners is revolutionizing coral reef restoration. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Coral Gardeners: Restoring the reef - Episode 2

title.value: Coral Gardeners: Restoring the reef - Episode 2

text.value: Since 2017, the Coral Gardeners collective has restored and monitored more than 30,000 corals. Founder Titouan Bernicot has no formal training in marine biology but the reef is his life. The organization has recruited experts and enthusiastic people willing to help. As a result, the team refines its methods and plants more corals every year. All the while, it is telling the “story of the reef” and has already reached more than 200 million people worldwide.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

metadescription.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

pageCaption.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

metadescription.value: 25 years after the creation of the Galápagos Marine Reserve, an expedition has been launched to collect new data. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

metadescription.value: Using eDNA, researchers are aiming to characterize the marine diversity of the Galápagos to protect specific habitats. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: The Coral Gardeners team keeps an eye on the future. As well as planting more and more corals every year, it is developing ReefOS, an artificial intelligence platform that includes automated sensors and cameras. This makes it possible to monitor coral nurseries and reefs, and track the restoration process. The organization’s target is to become “the biggest and most advanced coral restoration programme on the planet”.

metadescription.value: Through innovative technology and community engagement, Coral Gardeners is investing in the future of reef restoration. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Coral Gardeners: Restoring the reef - Episode 3

pageCaption.value: At 16 years of age, while surfing the crystal waters of his home island in French Polynesia, Titouan Bernicot had an eye-opening experience that led him to dedicate his life to protecting the coral reefs he loves. He founded Coral Gardeners, kick-starting coral restoration on the island alongside his childhood friends. Today these “ocean kids” are inspiring a global community of reef defenders.

metadescription.value: Discover the story of Titouan Bernicot, founder of Coral Gardeners, who has dedicated his life to restoring coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.
